description:
**Problem**
bd-3m5.20 is too large. Split: local cache for fast startup.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §13.8 step 8 (cache publish)
- Refines bd-3m5.20

**Design**
- Store verified copy under store_dir/checkpoint_cache/<group>/<checkpoint_id>/
- Atomic publish: write to .tmp/, fsync, rename, update CURRENT
- Keep last N checkpoints per group (default N=3), prune older
- CURRENT file points to newest verified checkpoint_id

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Cache written atomically with tmp+rename
- [ ] CURRENT updated after successful publish
- [ ] Pruning keeps only N most recent
- [ ] Startup reads from cache if available

**Files:** src/git/checkpoint/cache.rs
title: Phase 6: Checkpoint local cache with atomic publish

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All child tasks complete
- [ ] Apply/merge deterministic and order-independent
- [ ] D1 self-contained ops enforced
- [ ] OR-Set labels/deps + orphan notes/labels/deps live
- [ ] Legacy full-set / LWW dep paths deleted
description:
**Problem**
We must implement locked CRDT decisions D1–D5. The current system uses full-set LWW for labels, LWW dep edges with provenance, notes embedded in beads, and collision errors/left-wins. Apply is not total and order-dependent.

**Design**
Implement the plan in CRDT_OVERHAUL.md. This epic decomposes the work into independent tasks with strict dependency ordering.

**Files**
CRDT_OVERHAUL.md
design: Follow CRDT_OVERHAUL.md as the source-of-truth design plan. Each child task implements a scoped slice (orset core, store meta, wire ops, apply, mutation engine, git wire, cleanup).
title: CRDT overhaul: OR-Set labels/deps, orphan notes, deterministic collisions

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `bd migrate to 1` preserves/infers `root_slug` from existing store IDs when meta is missing the slug.
- [ ] The same legacy store migrates to the same `root_slug` in different clone contexts.
- [ ] Tests cover migration-specific slug inference without regressing `from-go` behavior.
description:
**Problem**
When `meta.root_slug` is absent, hard-cutover migration currently derives a new slug from repo context instead of inferring it from existing bead IDs. That makes the migrated metadata clone-dependent: the same legacy store can acquire different `root_slug` values depending on where migration is run.

The design explicitly prefers existing-bead inference so migration preserves repo identity independent of local clone path or remote URL normalization.

**Files**: crates/beads-git/src/sync.rs, crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/migration.rs, docs/plans/2026-03-01-legacy-deps-hard-cutover-design.md
design:
**Design**
Add a migration-only root-slug inference path that derives the slug from the canonical state when `meta.root_slug` is missing. Use repo-derived fallback only when the state does not provide a stable slug candidate.

Cover the behavior with migration tests that run the same legacy store through different clone contexts and assert identical migrated `root_slug` output.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Audit on 2026-03-11 against /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs-legacy-deps-v1-cutover @ 9aacf74f: hard-cutover migration no longer derives `root_slug` from clone-local repo context when metadata omits it. The migration path now preserves an existing meta slug when present, otherwise infers it from canonical bead/tombstone IDs, and only falls back to a stable default when the state provides no slug candidate. Coverage includes a migration-specific missing-root-slug test that rewrites legacy meta, migrates, and asserts the inferred slug comes from existing IDs rather than repo path. Verification: `cargo test -p beads-rs migration --features slow-tests -- --nocapture`, `cargo test`, `just dylint`, and `cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings` all pass.

title: Migration follow-up: infer missing root_slug from canonical state during cutover

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] WAL replay tracker uses Seq types end-to-end (no raw u64 for seq).
- [ ] WalReplayError variants carry `NamespaceId` + Seq types.
- [ ] Tests updated to cover the new typed errors/invariants.
description:
**Problem**
WAL replay code stores sequences as raw `u64` and error variants carry `namespace: String` + `seq: u64` (e.g., `WalReplayError::NonContiguousSeq`, `PrevShaMismatch`, `MissingHead`). `ReplayTracker`/`OriginReplayState` also use raw `u64`, forcing manual `seq == 0` checks and losing invariants that should be enforced by `Seq0`/`Seq1` and `NamespaceId`.

**Files**
- src/daemon/wal/replay.rs
design:
**Design**
- Refactor `ReplayTracker`/`OriginReplayState` to use `Seq0`/`Seq1` types for `max_seq`/`contiguous_seq` and comparisons.
- Update `WalReplayError` fields to use `NamespaceId` and typed seqs (`Seq0`/`Seq1`) instead of `String`/`u64`.
- Adjust error mapping/helpers and tests accordingly.
title: WAL replay should use typed seq/namespace in tracker + errors

description:
**Problem**

Go beads has StatusPinned for marking beads that should stay open indefinitely. Pinned beads are persistent context markers, not work items that need completion.

Missing from beads-rs:
- StatusPinned workflow status
- Pin/unpin commands
- Display icon (📌) and color
- Different cleanup behavior (pinned beads survive cleanup)

Pinned vs Deferred:
- Pinned: Stays open permanently, won't be auto-closed
- Deferred: Temporarily on ice, will be revisited

Use cases:
- Documentation beads that track ongoing decisions
- Reference beads for common patterns
- Context beads for project state

**Design**

Add StatusPinned variant to Status enum.

Commands:
- `bd pin [id]` - Set status to pinned
- `bd unpin [id]` - Restore to open

StatusPinned behavior:
- Shows in `bd list` but not `bd ready` (not actionable work)
- Excluded from cleanup operations (unlike closed/deferred)
- Can't be auto-closed by workflows
- Different from deferred: deferred will be revisited, pinned stays indefinitely

Display (from ui/styles.go):
- Icon: 📌 (pushpin emoji)
- Color: Purple (#d2a6ff) - special/elevated status
- Style: `StatusPinnedStyle.Render("📌")`

Implementation files:
- `src/core/workflow.rs` - Add StatusPinned variant
- `src/cli/pin.rs` - Pin command
- `src/cli/unpin.rs` - Unpin command (restore to open)
- `src/daemon/ops.rs` - Pin/unpin operations
- `src/cli/list.rs` - Display pinned beads with icon
- `src/cli/ready.rs` - Exclude pinned from ready work

**Design Notes**

Go beads has a `pinned` boolean field separate from status (types.go line 81). However, the Go codebase also has StatusPinned (line 447). Need to reconcile:
- Option 1: Status-based (StatusPinned) - simpler, no dual representation
- Option 2: Field-based (pinned bool) + status=open - allows "pinned in_progress"
- Recommendation: Use StatusPinned (status-based). Pinned beads aren't "work in progress", they're persistent markers.

Pinned beads don't expire:
- Cleanup operations skip pinned beads
- Can't be auto-closed by formulas/workflows
- Manual unpin required to close

**Acceptance**

- [ ] Status enum has StatusPinned variant
- [ ] `bd pin [id]` sets status to pinned
- [ ] `bd unpin [id]` restores to open
- [ ] `bd ready` excludes pinned beads
- [ ] Pinned status displays with 📌 icon in purple color
- [ ] Cleanup operations skip pinned beads
- [ ] Tests for pin/unpin operations
- [ ] Migration handles StatusPinned from Go beads export

**Files:**
- src/core/workflow.rs
- src/cli/pin.rs
- src/cli/unpin.rs
- src/daemon/ops.rs
- src/cli/list.rs
- src/cli/ready.rs
title: Pinned status for persistent beads
